summary:
The correct answer is "forms a gelatinous matrix" due to its ability to produce extracellular polymers like glucans during sugar metabolism. This matrix enhances microbial adherence and protects against environmental stressors such as antiseptic agents, contributing significantly to the persistence and pathogenicity of oral biofilms associated with tooth decay. 

The summary should reflect only what was directly reasoned through; do NOT add external knowledge.

Only include information present in Agent2’s response text above.

- Key new point introduced after tool call: forms a gelatinous matrix  

Then check where these are supported specifically:

In retrieval results section: “describes the dental biofilm...as having an organized structure including extracellular polysaccharides” – linked indirectly to bacteria producing them  
Specifically noted: “S. mutans synthesize glucan-based exopolymers derived from sucrose metabolism”, serving as part of the gelatinous matrix  
Also emphasized function: improves adhesion/stability & resistance to antimicrobial treatments
